g.text: 802 Bankview Dr, Drumheller, Alberta | File VAL251026
Alberta’s economy in 2025 continues to navigate the familiar tension between opportunity and volatility. Growth is
moderating toward the 2% range after stronger gains in earlier years, as energy markets stabilize and consumer
spending softens. Inflation has eased back near 2.4%, giving households some relief after the price spikes of 2022,
but higher borrowing costs and cautious hiring leave unemployment near 7%. On the positive side, housing demand
remains resilient, with new construction activity elevated to meet the needs of a fast-growing population that just
crossed five million. Energy prices for WTI and WCS remain within a supportive range, while natural gas markets
anticipate upside from future LNG projects. Large construction initiatives in petrochemicals, carbon capture, and
urban development add to the momentum. Still, Alberta’s reliance on commodity cycles means fiscal balances and
growth prospects remain vulnerable to price swings and external shocks. In sum, Alberta’s 2025 outlook reflects
steady growth, tight housing markets, and resource-driven stability, tempered by labour market slack and trade
